name: Machine Learning
description: The study of algorithms that improve automatically through experience and data.

name: Supervised Learning
description: Learning from labeled input-output pairs to predict outcomes on new data.

name: Unsupervised Learning
description: Finding hidden patterns in data without labeled examples.

name: Reinforcement Learning
description: Learning optimal behavior through trial-and-error interaction with an environment.

name: Optimization
description: Techniques for finding the best parameters to minimize or maximize an objective function.

name: Statistics
description: Mathematical methods for collecting and analyzing data to draw conclusions.

name: Linear Regression
description: Predicting a continuous output as a linear combination of input features.

name: Logistic Regression
description: Classification by estimating probabilities with a sigmoid function.

name: Decision Trees
description: Tree-structured models that split data on feature thresholds to make predictions.

name: Support Vector Machines
description: Classification by finding the optimal hyperplane that separates classes with maximum margin.

name: K-Nearest Neighbors
description: Classifying data points based on the majority class of their closest neighbors.

name: Naive Bayes
description: Probabilistic classifier using Bayes theorem with feature independence assumptions.

name: Entropy
description: A measure of uncertainty or information content in a probability distribution.

name: Cross-Entropy
description: A loss function measuring the difference between predicted and true probability distributions.

name: Deep Learning
description: Neural networks with multiple hidden layers that learn hierarchical representations.

name: Neural Networks
description: Computational models composed of layers of interconnected nodes inspired by biological neurons.

name: Perceptron
description: The simplest neural network unit that computes a weighted sum with a threshold activation.

name: Activation Functions
description: Nonlinear functions applied to neuron outputs to introduce representational power.

name: Backpropagation
description: Algorithm for computing gradients through the network to update weights via the chain rule.

name: Convolutional Neural Networks
description: Networks using convolutional filters to detect spatial patterns in grid-structured data.

name: Image Classification
description: Assigning a category label to an entire image based on its visual content.

name: Computer Vision
description: Enabling machines to interpret and understand visual information from images and video.

name: Generative Adversarial Networks
description: Two networks competing to generate increasingly realistic synthetic data.

name: Diffusion Models
description: Generating data by learning to reverse a gradual noising process.

name: Recurrent Neural Networks
description: Networks with loops that process sequential data by maintaining hidden state over time.

name: Transformers
description: Attention-based architecture that processes sequences in parallel for powerful modeling.

name: Attention
description: Mechanism allowing models to focus on the most relevant parts of the input dynamically.

name: Natural Language Processing
description: Enabling computers to understand and generate human language.

name: Word Embeddings
description: Dense vector representations of words that capture semantic relationships.

name: Language Models
description: Models that predict the probability of word sequences to generate or understand text.

name: BPE / Tokenization
description: Breaking text into subword units to build a compact vocabulary for language models.

name: BERT
description: Bidirectional transformer trained on masked language modeling for understanding tasks.

name: GPT
description: Autoregressive transformer that generates text by predicting the next token.

name: RLHF
description: Fine-tuning language models with human preference feedback via reinforcement learning.

name: Prompt Engineering
description: Designing effective input prompts to guide language model behavior and outputs.

name: Retrieval-Augmented Generation
description: Combining language models with external knowledge retrieval for grounded generation.

name: Fine-Tuning
description: Adapting a pretrained model to a specific task by continuing training on task data.

name: Clustering
description: Grouping similar data points together without predefined labels.

name: Dimensionality Reduction
description: Reducing the number of features while preserving important structure in the data.
